== Notes for [[Item:Q106468|Q106468]] ==

From ''Banu Saleem'' Clan of ''Banu al-Hawāzin'' Tribe.

Source 1: http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Atikah_bint_Murrah

Source 2: http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hawazin

Source 3: http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Abd_Manaf_ibn_Qusai

----

https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Atikah_bint_Murrah

----

===Biography

https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Atikah_bint_Murrah

'''ʿĀtikah bint Murrah ibn Hilāl ibn Fālij ibn Dhakwān''' (Arabic: عاتكة بنت مرة) (fl. 5th century CE) was a Hawazin heiress, and the mother of Hashim ibn 'Abd Manaf, thus the great-great-grandmother of Islamic prophet Muhammad.

===Family

She was daughter of Murrah ibn Hilal and Safiya bint Awf, cousin of Murrah. Atikah was a woman from Banu Hawazin. She married a leader of Banu Ka'b ibn Lu'ayy, Abd Manaf ibn Qusay. By him, she had many sons.

The sons are:
* al-Muttalib (المطلب)
* Amr/Hashim (عمرو/هاشم)
* Abd Shams (عبد الشمس)

The daughters are:
* Barrah (برة)
* Halah (هالة)
* Tumāḍir (تماضر)
* Qilabah (قلابة)
* Hayyah (حياة)
* Rayta (ريطة)
* Kathamah (خثامة)
* Sufyanah (سفيانة)

REFERENCE_NUMBERS:
geni:6000000003025011914

[[Category:GEDCOM Notes Pages]][[Category:Pages]]
